rationale: Keep: the citing paper explicitly studies cost-benefit heuristics including a price-quality/taste-efficacy inference, and the cited paper is a core prior result showing that price cues change perceived/actual efficacy via expectancies. Given this strong construct overlap (costlier price -> better outcomes) and the citing paper’s stated extension of prior cost-benefit heuristic work, this is plausibly a foundational/theory source rather than an incidental mention.
